purpose: Classifies the current cross-asset vol environment to determine positioning approach and risk management framework.
components: Regime A (Low-vol carry): All vol measures suppressed, positive carry environment, diversification works conventionally
Regime B (Synchronized expansion): Vol measures elevated but moving together, traditional correlations hold, systematic risk dominates
Regime C (Divergent vol — current): MOVE elevated, VIX suppressed, FX vol low; driven by Kalecki dual-channel where fiscal spending supports earnings while generating issuance uncertainty
current_assessment: Currently in Regime C (Divergent Vol). MOVE ~100-120, VIX ~13-18, G10 FX vol ~7-9%. Historically unstable with no precedent for persistence as steady state. All three historical analogues (2006-07, 2014, 2017-18) transitioned within 6-18 months.
limitations: Rigid at regime boundaries — transitions may not be cleanly categorized
Only three regimes may oversimplify the state space
Historical analogues have limited sample size
Does not distinguish between benign and violent resolution paths
